text.title: Facilitation Anti-Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules with rationale + source for facilitation-anti-patterns. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: If the sponsor speaks for more than five consecutive minutes inside an elicitation block, the BA interrupts with a written-poll round (sticky notes or chat) addressed to every other participant by name.
rule.rationale: Sponsor monologue research — applies to Facilitation Anti-Patterns.

rule.statement: Before any 'are we agreed?' close, the BA explicitly asks 'what would have to be true for this to be wrong?' and waits at least 15 seconds of silence before accepting the agreement.
rule.rationale: False-consensus anti-pattern catalogue — applies to Facilitation Anti-Patterns.

rule.statement: For every new question, every participant writes their answer privately for 90 seconds before any verbal sharing; the BA only reveals contributions after collection.
rule.rationale: First-speaker anchoring research — applies to Facilitation Anti-Patterns.

rule.statement: Every parking-lot item is tagged with an owner and a 'review by' timestamp inside the same session; any item older than two cycles without a decision is auto-escalated to the sponsor with a 'kill or schedule' choice — never silently dropped.
rule.rationale: Parking-lot graveyard anti-pattern — applies to Facilitation Anti-Patterns.

rule.statement: When the highest-paid person in the room overrides a workshop position, the BA writes the override into the minutes as 'decision by authority' with the named decider, the rejected alternatives, and the reason given — never as 'the group decided'.
rule.rationale: HiPPO / sponsor-override anti-pattern — applies to Facilitation Anti-Patterns.

rule.statement: If the conversation drifts off the published agenda for more than ten minutes, the BA stops and asks the room: 're-contract to the new topic and drop X, or park this and return to X?' — the room must vote before continuing.
rule.rationale: Workshop-drift anti-pattern — applies to Facilitation Anti-Patterns.

rule.statement: If the workshop has no power asymmetry, no contractual deliverable, and no historical anti-pattern, return `skipped`.
rule.rationale: Methodology applicability — applies to Facilitation Anti-Patterns.
